commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The remaining blocker is current-head Telegram proof or explicit maintainer acceptance; no narrow automated code repair is identified.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain concern found; the diff changes Telegram rendering logic, tests, and static display metadata without new dependencies, workflows, credentials, or permissions. (sha=24c8774c98d54d01f884adbded289b9a4c97bff5)
commands.review_summary:
This PR changes Telegram progress draft rendering to keep plain fallback text, attach escaped rich HTML for Telegram parse-mode transport, add API progress display metadata, and update Telegram/core regression tests.

PR surface: Source +18, Tests +133, Other +11. Total +162 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main still formats Telegram progress drafts through HTML/code wrappers and the linked issue points to existing Telegram progress tests that assert that shape. I did not run tests or live Telegram in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is current-head Telegram proof or explicit maintainer acceptance; no narrow automated code repair is identified.

target.title: fix(telegram): render progress draft rows as plain readable text
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #95002.

Telegram progress draft previews now keep their canonical preview text readable while preserving Telegram's native HTML transport for non-rich progress drafts.

## What Problem This Solves

The Telegram progress draft renderer used HTML/code-oriented progress rows as the primary draft representation. That made command/tool progress noisy when fallback or sanitized paths surfaced transport markup, and it left the `api` progress edge to generic fallback rendering.

## Changes

- Normalize Telegram progress draft rows through a plain readable line model first.
- Generate escaped Telegram HTML from the same normalized progress lines for transport.
- Preserve the default non-rich Telegram draft transport path: previews with `richMessage.html` are sent with `parse_mode: "HTML"`, with `TelegramDraftPreview.text` retained as the plain fallback if Telegram rejects the HTML parse.
- Remove the reverted `plainTextTransport` path and its test expectations.
- Map `itemKind: "api"` to the API tool display and add a first-class `API` display config.
- Update Telegram draft/dispatch regression coverage for readable text, preserved HTML transport, HTML-parse fallback, and both `api` progress shapes.

## Current-head behavior

At head `0df89a764914e0786060ae775c68687d4d29f28a`:

- `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts` returns `TelegramDraftPreview.text` as readable plain text and attaches escaped `richMessage.html` for Telegram transport.
- `extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.ts` sends attached HTML previews through `parse_mode: "HTML"` and keeps the plain text as fallback.
- `src/channels/streaming.ts` maps `itemKind: "api"` to `api`, and `src/agents/tool-display-config.ts` / the OpenClawKit resource snapshot include the API display entry.

## Evidence

GitHub Actions on current head `0df89a764914e0786060ae775c68687d4d29f28a`:

- `Workflow Sanity / no-tabs`: passed.
- `OpenGrep — PR Diff / Scan changed paths (precise)`: passed.
- `CodeQL` high-signal shards: passed.
- `CI / preflight`: passed.
- `CI / security-fast`: passed.
- `CI / build-artifacts`: passed.
- `src/agents/tool-display.test.ts`: passed inside `checks-node-core-fast`.
- `src/channels/streaming.test.ts`: passed inside `checks-node-core-fast`.
- `test/scripts/telegram-bot-api.test.ts`: passed inside `checks-node-core-tooling`.

Current broad CI has unrelated-looking failures outside this PR surface:

- `checks-node-core-fast`: `test/scripts/resolve-openclaw-ref.test.ts` expected resolver output but received `{}`.
- `checks-node-core-tooling`: `src/scripts/test-projects.test.ts` assertion drift and `test/scripts/bench-gateway-restart.test.ts` temp restart-intent ENOENT.
- `checks-node-agentic-agents-core-runner`: `src/agents/cli-runner.spawn.test.ts` hit `/tmp/mcp-captured.json` ENOENT plus a Claude live-session capacity unhandled rejection.

Those failures do not mention the Telegram progress draft files, `tool-display` snapshot, `telegramHtmlPreview`, or `plainTextTransport`. The earlier review-specific red checks are addressed on this head.

## Live Telegram proof status

A current-head Mantis proof has been requested with the maintainer-suggested command:

```text
@openclaw-mantis telegram desktop proof: verify progress draft rows show readable text while preserving Telegram HTML transport formatting.
```

This section should be updated with the Mantis artifact/result once the proof workflow finishes.

## Risk

Low-to-moderate Telegram presentation risk:

- This changes transient Telegram progress draft row formatting, not final answer delivery.
- Telegram HTML transport remains the default for attached progress preview HTML.
- Plain preview text is retained only as fallback when Telegram rejects HTML parsing.
- API progress now has an explicit display mapping instead of generic item fallback.

## Not addressed

This PR does not change Telegram draft materialization policy, including first-preview debounce/minimum length or progress-mode answer-lane suppression. That behavior remains separate from the progress-row formatting fix.
target.author: snowzlmbot
